Here's the Navbar component refactored to use only Tailwind CSS classes, removing all daisyUI-specific classes: ```tsx import { HiBolt, HiUserCircle } from "react-icons/hi2" import { Link, NavLink } from "react-router" const userNavigation = [ { name: "Home", href: "/", current: true }, { name: "Shifts", href: "/shifts", current: false }, ] const superUserNavigation = [ { name: "Home", href: "/", current: true }, { name: "Shifts", href: "/shifts", current: false }, { name: "Reports", href: "/reports", current: false }, { name: "Settings", href: "/settings", current: false }, ] export default function Navbar() { return (
) } export function MobileMenu() { const navigation = superUserNavigation return (
    {navigation.map((item) => (
  • `block px-4 py-2 text-sm hover:bg-gray-100 ${isActive ? "bg-gray-100" : ""}` } > {item.name}
    • ))}
) } export function AppLogo() { return (
P low
) } export function AppMenu() { const navigation = superUserNavigation return (
    {navigation.map((item) => (
  • `flex text-lg ${ isActive ? "font-semibold text-yellow-500 tracking-tight" : "tracking-tight hover:text-gray-600" }` } > {item.name}

Implemented dropdown functionality using positioning and visibility classes 4. Used Tailwind's built-in colors instead of daisyUI's theming system 5. Simplified the structure while maintaining the same functionality 6. Added hover and focus states using Tailwind classes The functionality remains the same, but now it only uses vanilla Tailwind CSS classes. You might need to add some JavaScript for toggle functionality of the dropdowns since we removed daisyUI's built-in dropdown behavior. The userIconDropdown is always visible, but you can add a state to toggle its visibility on click or hover. Here's the UserIconDropdown component updated with toggle functionality using React's useState hook: ```tsx export function UserIconDropdown() { const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false) return (
{isOpen && (
  • Profile New

)}
) } ``` You might also want to add a click outside handler to close the dropdown when clicking anywhere else on the page. Here's a more complete version with that functionality: ```tsx import { useState, useRef, useEffect } from 'react' export function UserIconDropdown() { const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false) const dropdownRef = useRef(null) useEffect(() => { function handleClickOutside(event: MouseEvent) { if (dropdownRef.current && !dropdownRef.current.contains(event.target as Node)) { setIsOpen(false) } } document.addEventListener("mousedown", handleClickOutside) return () => { document.removeEventListener("mousedown", handleClickOutside) } }, []) return (
{isOpen && (
  • setIsOpen(false)} > Profile New
  • setIsOpen(false)} > Settings
)}
) } ``` This version includes: 1. A state to control the dropdown's visibility 2. A click handler to toggle the dropdown 3. A click outside handler to close the dropdown when clicking elsewhere 4. onClick handlers on the menu items to close the dropdown when an item is selected You might also want to add a transition for a smoother open/close animation. Here's how you could add that: ```tsx {isOpen && (
    {/* ... rest of the dropdown content ... */}
)} ``` This will give you a smooth scaling and fade effect when the dropdown opens and closes. ```tsx plow-rr/app/components/layout/Footer.tsx import { HiHome, HiRectangleStack } from "react-icons/hi2" import { NavLink } from "react-router" const navigation = [ { name: "Home", href: "/", icon: HiHome }, { name: "Shifts", href: "/shifts", icon: HiRectangleStack }, // { name: "Settings", href: "/settings", icon: CogIcon }, ] export function Footer() { const links = navigation.map((item) => ( (isActive ? Here's the Footer component refactored to use only Tailwind CSS classes: Here's the Footer component refactored to use only Tailwind CSS classes: ```tsx import { HiHome, HiRectangleStack } from "react-icons/hi2" import { NavLink } from "react-router" const navigation = [ { name: "Home", href: "/", icon: HiHome }, { name: "Shifts", href: "/shifts", icon: HiRectangleStack }, // { name: "Settings", href: "/settings", icon: CogIcon }, ] export function Footer() { const links = navigation.map((item) => ( `flex flex-col items-center justify-center flex-1 px-2 py-1 ${isActive ? 'text-yellow-500' : 'text-gray-600 hover:text-gray-900'}` } > )) return (
{links}
) } ``` Key changes made: 1. Removed `btm-nav` and `btm-nav-md` classes and replaced them with appropriate flexbox and positioning classes 2. Removed `btm-nav-label` class and replaced it with basic text styling 3. Added hover states and active states using Tailwind classes 4. Added proper spacing and layout classes 5. Added backdrop blur effect similar to the navbar 6. Made the footer fixed to the bottom of the screen 7. Added proper flex layout for the navigation items The functionality remains the same, but now it uses only Tailwind CSS classes. The footer will stick to the bottom of the screen and display the navigation items in a row with icons and labels, highlighting the active route in yellow.
